response.justification: Bogdan brings a long, versatile engineering career with genuine high-throughput distributed-systems experience (micro-services handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic), current hands-on work building MCP code generators and agentic-workflow tooling at re:cinq, and strong TypeScript — a rare direct match for the AI-native mindset this role treats as baseline. However, the must-have language profile is inverted: he is strong in TypeScript with no Python evidence anywhere on the resume, his highest-scale distributed-systems work was in DLang and ended in 2020, and there is limited demonstrated experience directing technical strategy or managing cross-functional stakeholders at Staff scope. Worth a conversation if Python can be validated, but not a clean yes.
strengths: Current hands-on AI-native engineering experience (MCP code generators, agent infrastructure, agentic workflows)
Proven high-throughput distributed-systems work: low-latency micro-services at millions of HTTP requests/second
Broad full-stack range with strong TypeScript/Node.js and solid craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development)
concerns: No Python listed or evidenced anywhere, despite 'strong Python' being a must-have
Most intense distributed-systems experience is in DLang and dates to 2018–2020
Little concrete evidence of Staff-scope technical strategy, stakeholder coordination, or formal on-call/incident ownership
